resposta:
solution: ```python
import bisect

class MyCalendarThree:

    def __init__(self):
        self.starts = []
        self.ends = []
        self.k = 0

    def book(self, startTime: int, endTime: int) -> int:
        bisect.insort(self.starts, startTime)
        bisect.insort(self.ends, endTime)
        i = j = cur = 0
        max_k = 0
        while i < len(self.starts) and j < len(self.ends):
            if self.starts[i] < self.ends[j]:
                cur += 1
                max_k = max(max_k, cur)
                i += 1
            else:
                cur -= 1
                j += 1
        self.k = max(self.k, max_k)
        return self.k
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Uses two sorted lists to store start and end times; each insertion is O(log n) and a linear scan computes the maximum overlap in O(n). Maintains the global maximum k across calls.
